title: Bearing clearance arrangement in a tricone bit
abstract: A leg assembly (102) of a tricone bit includes a cone and a leg fitting inside the cone. The leg includes an axis (232) defining an axial center of rotation of the cone, a first raceway for a first row of bearings (202), a second raceway for a second row of bearings (206), and a third raceway for a third row of bearings (210) disposed between the cone and the leg, at a distal end (204), at a middle section (208), and at a proximal end (212) of the leg. A first clearance for the first row of bearings for the first raceway is less than a second clearance for the second row of bearings for the second raceway, which is less than a third clearance for the third row of bearings for the third raceway, such that the first row of bearings initially supports most of the radial load applied to the tricone bit.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A leg assembly (102, 104, 106) of a tricone bit (100) comprising: a cone (110); and a leg (200) fitting inside the cone (110), the leg (200) having an axis (232) defining an axial center of rotation of the cone (110), the leg (200) comprising: a first raceway (318) disposed between the cone (110) and the leg (200) at a distal end (204) of the leg (200) for a first row of bearings (202), a second raceway (322) disposed between the cone (110) and the leg (200) at a middle section (208) of the leg (200) for a second row of bearings (206), and a third raceway (326) disposed between the cone (110) and the leg (200) at a proximal end (212) of the leg (200) for a third row of bearings (210), wherein: a first clearance (408) associated with the first row of bearings (202) for the first raceway (318) is less than a second clearance (604) associated with the second row of bearings (206) for the second raceway (322), the second clearance (604) is less than a third clearance (704) associated with the third row of bearings (210) for the third raceway (326), and the first row of bearings (202) supports, in the first raceway (318), a radial load applied to the tricone bit (100) during a drilling process. 2. The leg assembly (102, 104, 106) of claim 1, wherein the second row of bearings (206) distributedly supports the radial load with the first row of bearings (202) in response to at least one of: the radial load applied to the tricone bit (100) is increased, or the first row of bearings (202) wearing down. 3. The leg assembly (102, 104, 106) of claim 2, wherein the third row of bearings (210) distributedly supports the radial load with the first row of bearings (202) and the second row of bearings (206) in response to at least one of: the radial load applied to the tricone bit (100) is further increased, or the first row of bearings (202) and the second row of bearings (206) wearing down. 4. The leg assembly (102, 104, 106) of claim 1, wherein: the first clearance (408) is 50 pm Â± 25 pm, the second clearance (604) is 150 pm Â± 25 pm, and the third clearance is (704) 250 pm Â± 25 pm. 5. A leg assembly (102, 104, 106) of a tricone bit (100) comprising: a leg (200) comprising: a first row of bearings (202), a second row of bearing (206), a third row of bearings (210), an axis defining an axial center of rotation (232), a first inner raceway (220) disposed at a distal end (204) of the leg (200), a second inner raceway (222) disposed at a middle section (208) of the leg (200), and a third inner raceway (224) at a proximal end (212) of the leg (200); and a cone (110) rotatably mounted on the leg (200) to rotate about the axis (232), the cone (200) comprising: 

 a first outer raceway (310) having a first outer diameter (316) and forming a first raceway (318) with the first inner raceway (220) for the first row of bearings (202), a second outer raceway (312) having a second outer diameter (320) and forming a second raceway (322) with the second inner raceway (222) for the second row of bearings (206), and a third outer raceway (314) having a third outer diameter (324) and forming a third raceway (326) with the third inner raceway (224) for the third row of bearings (210), the third outer diameter (324) smaller than the first outer diameter (316), wherein a first clearance (408) associated with the first row of bearings (202) for the first raceway (318) is less than a second clearance (604) associated with the second row of bearings (206) for the second raceway (322), and the second clearance (604) is less than a third clearance (704) associated with the third row of bearings (210) for the third raceway (326), and wherein, in response to a radial load applied to the tricone bit (100) during a drilling process, the first row of bearings (202) contacts the first inner raceway (220) and the first outer raceway (310), and supports the radial load. 6. The leg assembly (102, 104, 106) of claim 5, wherein the second row of bearings (206) and the third row of bearing (210) progressively and distributedly support the radial load by: the second row of bearings (206) contacting the second inner raceway (222) and the second outer raceway (312), and the third row of bearings (210) contacting the third inner raceway (224) and the third outer raceway (314). 7. The leg assembly (102, 104, 106) of claim 6, wherein the second row of bearings (206) and the third row of bearing (210) progressively and distributedly support the radial load in response to at least one of 

 the radial load applied to the tricone bit (100) is increased, or the first row of bearings (202) and the second row of bearings (206) wearing down. 8. A tricone bit (100) comprising three leg assemblies (102, 104, 106), each leg assembly (102, 104, 106) comprising: a cone (110); and a leg (200) fitting inside the cone (110), the leg (200) having an axis (232) defining an axial center of rotation of the cone (110), the leg (200) comprising: a first raceway (318) disposed between the cone (110) and the leg (200) at a distal end (204) of the leg (200) for a first row of bearings (202), a second raceway (322) disposed between the cone (110) and the leg (200) at a middle section (208) of the leg (200) for a second row of bearings (206), and a third raceway (326) disposed between the cone (110) and the leg (200) at a proximal end (212) of the leg (200) for a third row of bearings (210), wherein: a first clearance (408) associated with the first row of bearings (202) for the first raceway (318) is less than a second clearance (604) associated with the second row of bearings (206) for the second raceway (322), the second clearance (604) is less than a third clearance (704) associated with the third row of bearings (210) for the third raceway (326), and the first row of bearings (202) supports, in the first raceway (318), a radial load applied to the tricone bit (100) during a drilling process. 9. The tricone bit (100) of claim 8, wherein the second row of bearings (206) and the third row of bearing (210) progressively and distributedly support the radial load by: 

 the second row of bearings (206) contacting a second inner raceway (222) and a second outer raceway (312) of the second raceway (322), and the third row of bearings (210) contacting a third inner raceway (224) and a third outer raceway (314) of the third raceway (326). 10. The tricone bit (100) of claim 8, wherein: the first clearance (408) is 50 m Â± 25 pm, the second clearance (604) is 150 pm Â± 25 pm, and the third clearance is (704) 250 pm Â± 25 pm.
